record.text: How can you not love the mailbag?

images.alt:
Q: Two years ago, I drove across the country to Speedway, Ind. IMS, Purdue and NASA held a solar eclipse watch party at IMS. As part of the event, Ed Carpenter did a four-lap qualifying demonstration in an IndyCar. Did this count towards the team’s testing allotment for the year, or was it exempt because it was a special event?

Reginald L., San Diego, CA

MP: As Bill Simmons often wrote in his ESPN mailbag, you can’t make this stuff up…

This wins the "Most Random Question Award" of the year. Answering this definitively would require an outreach to Chevy, and a guaranteed response of, “Are you effing serious, Pruett… don’t you have anything better to do with your time?”

And then I’d say, “Well, yes, but this is actually from Reginald in San Diego,” and they’d say, “Sure it is…” and then they’d hang up. So I’ll save myself from that embarrassment and just go with an answer of no.

record.text: Before people get crazy with fan fest speculation over the minions, I wanted to note that minions aren't necessarily indicative of what the expansion will be about. EW using IV theming was accurate, but SHB fan fest had VII minions and a mount.

images.alt: The 2016–2017 Fan Festival previewed the Stormblood expansion. The battle challenge was an in-development version of Proto Ultima in a special instanced arena in Azys Lla. The in-game reward for attending was an option of the Abes Attire Set or the High Summoner's Attire Set, as well as one of three minions from Final Fantasy X, depending on which event or digital stream was purchased. They were Wind-up Rikku, Wind-up Lulu, and Wind-up Yuna for North America, Japan, and Europe, respectively.

The 2018–2019 Fan Festival previewed the Shadowbringers expansion. The battle challenge was an in-development version of the Kugane Ohashi fight against Yojimbo. The 2018 Feast Regional Championships took place at each event. The in-game reward for attending was the SDS Fenrir, as well as one of three minions from Final Fantasy VII, depending on which event or digital stream was purchased. They were Wind-up Tifa, Wind-up Cloud, and Wind-up Aerith for North America, Europe, and Japan, respectively.

The 2020–2021 Fan Festival events were cancelled due to the COVID-19 pandemic. They were originally scheduled for November 6–7, 2020 at the San Diego Convention Center in San Diego, California, December 19–20, 2020 at Aichi Sky Expo in Nagoya, Japan, and February 20–21, 2021 at ExCeL London in London, UK. In their place, the 2021 Digital Fan Festival Around the World was streamed for free from May 14–15, 2021 to preview the Endwalker expansion. No tickets were sold; instead certain new items were launched on the Final Fantasy XIV Online Store with a limited time discount during the event including the Lunar Whale, Wind-up Rydia, Wind-up Edge, Wind-up Rosa, Scions & Sinners: Piano Orchestrion Roll Set, Scions & Sinners: Band Orchestrion Roll Set, and Pulse Orchestrion Roll Set I.

images.alt:
A close-up selfie of mom and I sitting together at an outdoor event on a bright, sunny day. Our faces fill most of the frame, leaning in closely, cheek-to-cheek, both smiling warmly at the camera.

My mom on the left wears her favorite reflective aviator sunglasses and a black jacket. Her hair is styled with bangs across her forehead, and her smile is wide and relaxed. The reflection in her sunglasses shows hints of the crowd and surroundings.

I am on the right with my short, natural Sisterlocks and a bright, open smile. She leans in with a sense of ease and closeness, her expression joyful and present.

Behind them, rows of people are seated closely together at the kickoff of the Chancellor’s Associates Scholars Program that would fully pay for my undergraduate education at the University of California San Diego. The sunlight is strong, casting clear shadows and highlighting the warmth of the day. Many people in the background appear relaxed and engaged, contributing to a feeling of shared public joy and normalcy.

Above the image is a social media post reading: 

“Christine Evelyn Nolan with Syreeta Nolan
March 18, 2021
❤️❤️❤️ Yay for happy times without masks… can’t wait to get back to more normalcy,” 

This picture is from before the pandemic started and before what was normal was gone for the foreseeable future where we held a sense of togetherness, closeness, and ease that would soon become complicated. The photo reflects not only a shared joyful experience, but also a time when physical closeness in public spaces felt natural and unguarded.

This week is Long COVID Awareness Week, so we're taking a close look at this #ChronicDisease, which affects ~ 27% of COVID survivors.

#health #medicine #SanDiego #LaJolla #COVID19

images.alt:
Join us on March 19, 2026, for a Life Without Disease event featuring LJI Associate Professor Sonia Sharma, Ph.D., UC San Diego Health cardiologist Pam Taub, M.D., FACC, FASPC, and award-winning health journalist Jason Gale. Dive into the science of post-viral diseases and learn about the urgent efforts to help patients recover from long COVID. This panel discussion will be moderated by LJI Professor, President & CEO Erica Ollmann Saphire, Ph.D., MBA.

Join us after the panel for refreshments and a signing of Jason Gale's book: After Covid. The Health Impacts That Will Last Generations.

Join us after the panel for refreshments and a signing of Jason Gale's book: After Covid. The Health Impacts That Will Last Generations.

images.alt:
Curve Appeal: The Architecture and Engineering of Membrane Bending

This webinar, hosted by the Membrane Structure & Function Subgroup of the Biophysical Society, and organized by Adam Smith, will highlight how lipid composition, protein assemblies, and membrane mechanics work together to generate and regulate membrane curvature in cells. Three invited speakers will present on membrane bending in a way that's accessible to undergraduate students interested in biochemistry and biophysics, with sufficiently many fun new things sprinkled in to also be interesting to specialists in the field.

Invited speakers are:
   Itay Budin (UC San Diego)
   Margaret E. Johnson (Johns Hopkins University)
   Cecília Leal (University of Illinois Urbana–Champaign).

Sign-up link for the webinar:

https://my.biophysics.org/Events/Calendar-Of-Events/Meeting-Home-Page?meetingid=95b51c20-8219-f111-8342-7ced8d22117d

Please share widely!

external.description: Join KPBS for a special event celebrating the 100th episode of Ken Kramer’s About San Diego You’re invited for a special evening celebrating the 100th episode of Ken Kramer’s About San Diego. This live, in-person event honors his work in bringing the stories, people and places that make San Diego so special. Event highlights: - Sneak peek at episode 101, premiering on March 19 on KPBS-TV - Behind the scenes stories from Ken Kramer and show producer Suzanne Bartole - Q&A with Ken and Suzanne - Selfie station at the KPBS step & repeat - No host bar Event timeline: 6:00 p.m. | Pre Show Meet & Greet (exclusive to $120 ticket holders) 7:00 p.m. | Doors open for general admission - open seating 7:30 p.m. | On stage: Ken Kramer and Suzanne Bartole in conversation with KPBS 8:30 p.m. | Good night Your ticket supports KPBS and our commitment to local storytelling, history and voices. About the series For more than five decades, the Emmy-winning show Ken Kramer’s About San Diego has presented stories of the history, places and people of the area we call home. Ranging over all of San Diego County, the stories hosted by legendary Ken Kramer and co-produced by Suzanne Bartole share little known things about our past and how they relate to places we see every day. You learn something you might not have known about San Diego. Ken Kramer’s About San Diego airs on KPBS-TV every Thursday at 8:00 p.m. and can be streamed anytime on KPBS+.

images.alt: Color photo of one of many Chicano Park pillars covered in beautiful Chicano murals. This particular pillar, which reads "All the Way to the Bay" at the top, was painted by artist Victor Ochoa.

One of the miniature pillars inside the Chicano Park Museum which includes the mission and purpose of the San Diego-based cultural center. Painted in black on this cream-colored pillar are images that can be found outside in Chicano Park.

According to the museum caption, Ramsés Noriega created this announcement for a 1976 rally in Los Angeles featuring speakers Bishop Juan Alfredo Arzube and Congressman Edward Roybal. The artist used his own green card as the format to announce this event at the Embassy Auditorium.

record.text:
Ce soir, un communiqué de la NASA à la demande de l'astronaute Mike Fincke nous apprend qu'il était à l'origine de l'incident médical qui a demandé un retour anticipé de Crew-11.

Il insiste sur le fait que ce n'était pas une urgence mais qu'il valait mieux profiter de l'imagerie médicale sur Terre.

images.alt:
NASA
"On Jan. 7, while aboard the International Space Station, I experienced a medical event that required immediate attention from my incredible crewmates. Thanks to their quick response and the guidance of our NASA flight surgeons, my status quickly stabilized.
“After further evaluation, NASA determined the safest course was an early return for Crew-1 for Crew-11 — not an emergency, but a carefully coordinated plan to be able to take advantage of advanced medical imaging not available on the space station. On Jan. 15, we splashed down off the coast of San Diego after an amazing five-and-a-half-month mission.
"I am deeply grateful to my fellow Expedition 74 members - Zena Cardman, Kimiya Yui, Oleg Platonov, Chris Williams, Sergey Kud-Sverchkov, and Sergei Mikayev - as well as the entire NASA team, SpaceX, and the medical professionals at Scripps Memorial Hospital La Jolla near San Diego. Their professionalism and dedication ensured a positive outcome.
"I'm doing very well and continuing standard post-flight reconditioning at NASA's Johnson Space Center in Houston. Spaceflight is an incredible privilege, and sometimes it reminds us just how human we are. Thank you all for your support."

value.text: I voted for Jesse Jackson in 1988, and was inspired by his vision for a multiracial America embodied in the Rainbow Coalition. I have never forgotten his injunction to “keep hope alive!”, especially during these dark times. In his honor, I intend to keep hope alive. Rest in Power, Rev. Jackson.

Scientists discover the enzyme that lets cancer rapidly rewire its DNA

Scientists at the University of California San Diego have identified the enzyme responsible for chromothripsis, a dramatic genetic event in which a chromosome breaks into many fragments and is stitched back together in the…

external.description: Scientists at the University of California San Diego have identified the enzyme responsible for chromothripsis, a dramatic genetic event in which a chromosome breaks into many fragments and is stitched back together in the wrong order. This chaotic reshuffling allows cancer cells to evolve quickly and develop resistance to treatment. Although chromothripsis was first recognized more than ten years ago as a major force in cancer progression, researchers had not known what actually set it in motion. The new findings, published in Science, uncover the cause and point to potential new strategies for treating some of the most aggressive cancers.
external.title: Scientists discover the enzyme that lets cancer rapidly rewire its DNA

Who pegged Rand Paul as the moderating voice on the Ice and Border Protection hearing?
www.c-span.org/event/senate...

Also, Commissioner Rodney Scott gives the appearance of a cool level headed leader. A look at his Wiki shows something different.

images.alt: Former CBP deputy assistant commissioner James Wong testified that, while Scott was San Diego Sector chief, he “supervised” a Critical Incident Team’s handling of Anastasio Hernández Rojas’s beating and tasing by Border Patrol agents. Scott allegedly used an administrative subpoena to obtain medical records during an active criminal probe, delayed notifications to local authorities, mishandled evidence, and allowed key video footage to be erased. Although the Justice Department declined to press charges, a wrongful-death suit was settled for $1 million in 2017. Senator Ron Wyden has since subpoenaed DHS records to examine Scott’s role.[9][10]

Rape threat tweet

In September 2021, Scott sent a tweet to former senior Border Patrol agent and whistleblower in Hernández Rojas case Jenn Budd that a San Diego Superior Court judge described as a “classic" rape threat. During his April 2025 confirmation hearing, Scott apologized, calling it a “weak moment” and insisting he meant to challenge her “creative imagination” about her allegations—an explanation that stunned senators and watchdogs.[15]
Facebook group

Reports surfaced in June 2025 that Scott was part of a closed Border Patrol Facebook group where agents joked about migrant deaths and posted racist and misogynistic memes.[16] Scott claimed to have joined the group in order to keep an eye on what other agents were doing and said that nothing he saw rose to the level of reportable conduct. An internal investigation by CBP found 60 agents to have committed misconduct in the group.[17] He has publicly distanced himself from those posts, saying he never contributed such content.[10]

record.bridgyOriginalText:
We are very excited for next week's Shading Language Symposium in San Diego!

This event will bring together graphics and computer shader programmers, researchers and technical artists with shading language implementers to explore the landscape of shading languages, their future development, and the role how new techniques such as neural graphics will play in shaping the future of real-time rendering.

Learn more: https://www.khronos.org/events/shading-languages-symposium-2026
#glsl #hlsl #osl #slang #spirv #wgsl

Weekly report: 203 teachers signed up at ZEP for free people’s history lessons from Flagstaff, AZ; LA & SD, CA; Lafayette, IN; Boston, MA; St Paul, MN; Cleveland, OH; Middleburg, VA; & many more cities. 

Some reasons why. ⬇️

Donate so we can reach more classrooms: www.zinnedproject.org/donate/

images.alt:
To access lesson materials for the American Revolution unit. — Lafayette, Indiana
A friend just told me about this site for teaching U.S. history. — Middleburg, Virginia
I am looking for resources to share with teachers so we can do some teach-ins on important issues such as immigration and how people organize to fight back. — Los Angeles, California
The resources I have found at the ZEP site are nothing short of inspiring and I hope to continue to stay up to date, informed, and responsible about the content I use with my diverse classroom of students. — Cleveland, Ohio

I am registering to utilize these rich resources for my classroom. — Saint Paul, Minnesota
I’m trying to keep myself knowledgeable and grounded through all of the impending chaos. — Flagstaff, Arizona
I want to keep the high schoolers I work with informed on the ties between history and the events occurring today. — San Diego, California

This event is next Weds 2/04, California Center for the Arts, Escondido. This is the San Diego homeless choir. Rehearse and feed people every week. 

Consider catching one of their shows, free or paid, or donating if that's your thing. Joy is the best healer.

images.alt:
Voices of Our City Choir is the homeless choir in San Diego. They rehearse and feed ppl every week. Come to their concert in Escondido next Weds Feb 4th at 7pm. California Center for the Arts, Escondido.

"Join us for an evening of powerful, uplifting music as the Voices of Our City Choir presents Celebration of Black Joy, a journey through inspirational songs that honor resilience, community, and shared humanity. Presented as part of CCAE First Wednesdays, this free concert brings together voices and stories that reflect the strength and creativity of our city. Through music rooted in lived experience, the choir celebrates joy, connection, and the power of coming together."

I know @dgoold.bsky.social has visited this topic numerous times in chats and on podcasts: fan atmosphere inside Busch III.

This passage crystallizes the issue. 

https://www.stltoday.com/sports/professional/mlb/cardinals/article_ca58a630-197f-407a-a509-1b6d3cedbc32.html

images.alt:
A reminder of what the Cardinals missed at home this past season actually happened on the road. 

A young player on his team approached manager Oli Marmol in the dugout at a raucous, buzzing ballpark and described how he enjoyed playing on the road more than in St. Louis. Marmol called the moment “heartbreaking.” He declined to identify the player or the opponent, though it could have been Philadelphia or Wrigley or San Diego — anywhere with a packed house and the crackle of contention to contrast with the uncharacteristic calm of empty sections at Busch Stadium.

Marmol told this story Friday night during an event at Lindenwood University as a way to convey to the Cardinals fans present the role they play in bringing out the best in players and also bringing the best players home to Busch.

To bring back the best of Busch.

“For that young core to feel that,” Marmol told the crowd Friday night, “might be the most important thing ever.”

record.text: Very excited for the Imaginarium Symposium today.

images.alt: Poster for “Imaginarium,” a one-day symposium presented by UC San Diego Center for Psychedelic Research, the Arthur C. Clarke Center for Human Imagination, and MINDS. Event date January 17, 2026, 9AM–5PM at The Jeannie, UC San Diego, with streaming available. Description notes psychedelics catalyzing imagination, creative breakthroughs, and design at the intersection of neuroscience, creativity, and technology. Speakers listed include Hamilton Morris, Alex Grey, Allyson Grey, Rick Doblin, Kalina Christoff, James Fadiman, Bruce Damer, Manesh Girn, Cassandra Vieten, Jon Dean, Nick Denomme, Shane Mauss, Tracy Deluca, and others. Background is a pastel gradient blending pink, blue, and peach.

Detailed schedule for the Imaginarium symposium. Timeline from 8:30AM doors open to 5:00PM closing. Sessions include welcome remarks, talks on psychedelics and creativity, MDMA research, mind-wandering and hypnosis panel, LSD and creativity, psychedelic assisted therapy, Hamilton Morris keynote, audiovisual excerpt “Creality,” microdosing talk by James Fadiman, design in the psychedelic ecosystem, psilocin and synaptic learning, DMT and visionary states panel with Alex Grey and Allyson Grey, and imaginative play panel. Asterisks indicate virtual presentations. Background uses soft pastel color bands.

painting by Alex Grey (2005) of a hummingbird hovering near purple morning-glory flowers. The bird is shown semi-transparent with anatomical details visible and a glowing star symbol at its chest. Radiating rainbow energy lines surround the wings against a bright blue, web-like background. Leaves and blossoms frame the scene, giving a luminous, psychedelic, spiritual aesthetic.
